title: Don't be deceived by A.I.
description: Apostle Quentin Cook counsels BYU students and Latter-day Saints about the dangers of artificial intelligence. Let technology be "a servant," he said, "not a master."
content:
In this emerging age of artificial intelligence, it will become increasingly easy to be deceived and steered away from truth, an apostle of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints warned Tuesday.
Quentin L. Cook counseled Brigham Young Univer... [3039 chars]

title: China's economic ambitions hit limits to growth as its national congress prepares to meet
description: The gap between Chinese leader Xi Jinping's high-tech, artificial intelligence-driven ambitions and the hard realities of slowing growth is the backdrop for the annual meeting of the country’s largely ceremonial national legislature, the National People’s Congress, which begins Thursday.
content: BEIJING (AP) — China's progress in building a modern economy, evident in its kung-fu fighting robots and self-parking cars, is hitting limits as a downturn in its housing industry drags on, small businesses suffer and young people struggle to find jo... [7041 chars]

title: SMEs see artificial intelligence potential but ‘fail to capitalise on new tech’
description: Fear of making mistakes, lack of skills and cost behind reluctance to adopt new technology
content:
The majority of small and medium-sized businesses (SMEs) believe artificial intelligence (AI) can transform their business for the better, but a fear of making mistakes and a lack of skills are hampering adoption, notes fresh research.

title: Here’s how AI translates brain signals into words
description: Artificial intelligence and neuroscience are getting closer to making human thoughts speak and show themselves in images. This gives new hope to people suffering from paralysis or speech disorders....

title: AI could be giving US lethal edge in Iran war - but there are dangers
description: Artificial intelligence can parse vast amounts of data and use it to flag targets, rank threats and suggest priorities. But experts are worried human oversight could be eroded to a dangerous form of "rubber stamping".
content:
Forget science fiction. The age of AI in war is here.
Israel has used AI systems in Gaza to flag potential targets and help prioritise operations.
The United States military reportedly used Anthropic's model, Claude, during its operation to abduct Ni... [5711 chars]

title: X to impose 90-day revenue ban for undisclosed AI-generated videos
description: The announcement comes amid concerns over the misleading visuals during wartime, particularly as artificial intelligence tools make it easier to generate realistic but doctored footage.
content: Social media platform X has tightened its Creator Revenue Sharing rules, announcing new penalties for users who post undisclosed AI-generated content related to armed conflicts. Nikita Bier, head of product at X, said the changes will protect the aut... [1334 chars]

title: Qualcomm CEO predicts 2026 will be the year of the AI agent
description: Qualcomm Inc. Chief Executive Officer Cristiano Amon, speaking this week at the MWC Barcelona conference, said the incoming tide of artificial intelligence agents will transform the wider digital ecosystem.

title: ASM’s Revenue Outlook Beats Estimates on AI Investments, China
description: ASM International NV forecast revenue for the first quarter that beat analysts’ estimates, boosted by artificial intelligence-linked investments and expectations of strong China demand.
content:

title: Canada courts Indian researchers amid US funding unease
description: Indian researchers are now looking at Canada for doctoral and postdoctoral studies. This shift is driven by concerns over research funding and visa stability in the United States. Canada's new initiatives signal clearer long-term research career prospects. Fields like artificial intelligence and healthcare are seeing this trend. This reassessment focuses on risk management and stable research environments.
content:
